summary: Resolved the ADR 0113 merge blockers in-repo by isolating per-service service_health probe faults, allowing maintenance_windows to use worker-local NATS credentials, and reconciling the Windmill runtime env contract. The repaired runtime was demonstrated live on docker-runtime-lv3, but a concurrent older automation path rewrote the worker checkout and Windmill runtime env/template back to the pre-fix state before the final steady-state replay could complete.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest tests/test_world_state_workers.py tests/test_world_state_repo_surfaces.py tests/test_maintenance_window_tool.py tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py tests/unit/test_world_state_materializer.py -q` passed with 30 tests, `python3 -m compileall config/windmill/scripts/world-state platform/world_state scripts/maintenance_window_tool.py` completed successfully, and `make syntax-check-windmill` passed.

verification.check: Windmill runtime recovery and repo sync
verification.observed: After the shared `converge-windmill` replay was blocked by the Windmill runtime collision, the ADR 0113 worker checkout was resynced onto `/srv/platform_server`, the OpenBao secret payload was repopulated with the full world-state/NATS/ledger/graph/test-runner superset, and the Windmill containers were recreated cleanly on `docker-runtime-lv3`.
On `postgres-lv3`, `windmill_user` was granted `USAGE` on schema `world_state` plus table and sequence privileges there, which unblocked inserts from the worker DSN used by ADR 0113.
The first live refresh populated `world_state.current_view`, and `SELECT ispopulated FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname='world_state' AND matviewname='current_view'` returned `t` afterward.

verification.check: Verified live surfaces before merge-blocker follow-up
verification.observed: Direct worker execution on `docker-runtime-lv3` succeeded for `proxmox_vms` (7 records), `container_inventory` (58), `netbox_topology` (0), `dns_records` (25), `tls_cert_expiry` (23), `opentofu_drift` (0), and `openbao_secret_expiry` (47).

`world_state.current_view` held seven fresh rows for `container_inventory`, `dns_records`, `netbox_topology`, `openbao_secret_expiry`, `opentofu_drift`, `proxmox_vms`, and `tls_cert_expiry` immediately after the replay.

The branch now prevents one bad HTTP/TLS/TCP probe from aborting `service_health`, allows `maintenance_windows` to use direct worker `LV3_MAINTENANCE_WINDOWS_NATS_URL` or `LV3_NATS_URL` credentials, keeps Windmill `runtime.env` OpenBao-owned, and uses nil-safe optional key lookups in `runtime.env.ctmpl`.

After restoring the full OpenBao secret payload and the repaired `runtime.env.ctmpl`, `/run/lv3-secrets/windmill/runtime.env` temporarily contained `LV3_GRAPH_DSN`, `WORLD_STATE_DSN`, `LV3_NATS_*`, `LV3_LEDGER_*`, `LV3_TEST_RUNNER_*`, `LV3_INTEGRATION_ENVIRONMENT`, and the recreated `windmill_worker` container carried those variables in its environment.

Before the final wrapper replay completed, `/opt/windmill/openbao/runtime.env.ctmpl`, `/run/lv3-secrets/windmill/runtime.env`, and `/srv/platform_server/platform/world_state/workers.py` were overwritten back to their older three-key / pre-fix variants by another live automation path, so the durable post-fix runtime could not be held long enough to capture a clean final replay from the repaired state.

notes: The original shared `make converge-windmill` replay and the later manual repairs both showed that ADR 0113 works when the Windmill runtime env contract is repaired, but another concurrent automation path kept restoring the pre-fix Windmill OpenBao template and worker checkout on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

The broader `make validate` run again reached the repo-wide `ansible-lint` phase and was interrupted after the focused ADR 0113 checks had already passed; that broad validation path still contains unrelated repo debt outside this ADR.

This branch is ready to merge so `main` becomes the canonical owner of the repaired Windmill runtime env/template contract and the maintenance/service-health fixes. The remaining live follow-up is to replay from `main` after the competing overwrite path is stopped.
